marketState: neutral-to-bearish — oversupplied storage and a broadly bearish technical trend outweigh a fading near-term weather spike
summary: Henry Hub front-month trades at $2.861, essentially at fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417) but well below its seasonal median (-12.6pp, -16% YTD vs -3.4% median), and low-reliability seasonality still tilts lower (38/38/50/31% higher over 1-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%). Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, reinforcing an oversupplied backdrop, while COT is net-short but only at the 35th percentile — not an extreme positioning skew. Technicals are uniformly bearish across every timeframe from 5-minute to weekly (EMA50<200, mostly negative MACD), though 4h RSI (22) and a 1d close below its lower Bollinger band flag an oversold, bounce-prone tape. Weather shows a strong near-term demand spike (1-7D +25.1% vs 5y, top of range, above max) that is already fading (24h change -7.1) and reverses to below-normal in the 8-16D window (-9.4%), so the bullish near-term catalyst looks short-lived. Net: fundamentals and trend favor the downside over 3-12 weeks, while the next 1-10 days are a genuine tug-of-war best played from the sidelines until price confirms direction.
